label: Knee tendinitis without rupture or infection red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated knee tendinitis.

No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, septic joint or infection concern, large effusion, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Tendon rupture, fracture or dislocation concern, septic joint or infection concern, large effusion, open wound, neurovascular compromise, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, therapy, urgent care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated knee tendinitis with rupture, fracture, septic joint, inability-to-bear-weight, and neurovascular red flags absent.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports tendinitis framing, overuse-related tendon pain, activity modification, clinician-directed pain control, and separation from rupture, infection, trauma, or neurovascular compromise. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated knee tendinitis with rupture, fracture, septic joint, inability-to-bear-weight, and neurovascular red flags absent.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis of uncomplicated knee tendinitis
intact neurovascular exam and able to bear weight
